channel.title: Frontion Geopolitical News
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news curated from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: 🇮🇷🇺🇸 US and Iran Signal Peace Deal Close as Military Action Continues Near Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Washington and Tehran indicated they are near an agreement to end their three-month conflict, though Iranian drones targeting commercial ships were downed in the Strait of Hormuz. A senior US official said both sides agreed on a text and expect to sign an initial deal in coming days.

item.title: 🇦🇿 Azerbaijan Sentences Prominent Rights Activist to Eight Years in Prison
item.description: A court in Baku convicted prominent rights activist of hooliganism and fraud in a case his lawyer called politically motivated. The sentencing raises concerns about human rights in the oil-rich Caucasus nation.

item.title: 🇨🇳💰 China Bank Lending Misses Forecasts as Property Slump Drags On
item.description: New bank lending in China rose less than expected in May after contracting the previous month, as a prolonged property downturn continued to weigh on household borrowing in the world's second-largest economy.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 US Deports Iranian Pro-Democracy Activist to Central African Republic
item.description: The United States deported an Iranian pro-democracy activist to Central African Republic, her lawyer said, describing it as a "super dangerous" transfer to a country with which the activist has no connection.

item.title: 🇺🇸⛪ US Evangelicals Divided on Trump's Iran War and Immigration Crackdown, Poll Shows
item.description: Evangelical Christians, who helped power Trump's 2024 election victory, are showing divisions over the president's war in Iran and immigration policies, according to a new Reuters/Ipsos poll.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukrainian drone strike kills one at Russian port near Black Sea
item.description: A Ukrainian drone attack killed one person and sparked a fire at a sea terminal in southern Russia's Temryuk port, in the Krasnodar region, as Ukraine continues targeting Russian infrastructure.

item.title: 🇻🇪 Trump says US military strike killed leader of Venezuela's Tren de Aragua gang
item.description: President Trump announced US forces carried out a strike that killed Hector Rusthenford Guerrero Flores, leader of the Venezuelan prison gang Tren de Aragua, with Venezuela calling it a joint operation.

item.title: 🇮🇷 US downs Iranian drones targeting commercial ships near Strait of Hormuz
item.description: US forces shot down multiple Iranian attack drones heading toward commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, even as Washington and Tehran signal they are close to reaching a peace agreement.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Mines Nuclear Site Entrance to Prevent US Uranium Recovery
item.description: Iran has deliberately collapsed tunnels and mined entrances to its bombed Isfahan nuclear complex to deter any US attempt to retrieve the uranium stockpile, according to Israeli sources.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇪🇺 US Military Assets in Europe Face Significant Cuts Under Trump
item.description: The Trump administration plans major reductions to US military presence in Europe, creating uncertainty for British defence planning as welfare spending is flagged to help fund shortfalls.

item.title: 🇪🇺 Kaja Kallas Faces Growing Criticism as EU Foreign Policy Chief
item.description: The EU's High Representative Kaja Kallas is under mounting pressure, with critics highlighting structural shortcomings and tensions in European foreign policy coordination.

item.title: 🇮🇳🇫🇷 India and France Deepen Strategic Partnership in Defence
item.description: India and France are strengthening their long-standing strategic partnership with a renewed 10-year defence pact, including deeper cooperation on undersea capabilities.

item.title: 🇮🇳🇫🇷 PM Modi Departs for France and Slovakia, G7 Summit on Agenda
item.description: Indian Prime Minister Modi begins a two-nation European visit with bilateral talks in France and Slovakia, ahead of G7 Summit participation.

item.title: 🇧🇷⚠️ Brazilian refineries sold fuel to firm linked to terrorist organization
item.description: Reuters reports Brazilian refineries sold over 100 million liters of naphtha to a firm under investigation for fraud involving a U.S.-designated terrorist group, raising concerns about supply chain integrity.

item.title: 🏛️⚖️ Kennedy Center misses court deadline to remove Trump name
item.description: The Kennedy Center asked for more time to remove President Trumps name from its facade after missing a Friday deadline, citing thunderstorms. Officials say removal expected by Saturday noon.

item.title: 🇷🇺⚔️ Over 225,000 Russian soldiers identified killed in Ukraine
item.description: Russian independent media Mediazona, working with BBC Russian service, confirmed identities of over 225,000 Russian soldiers killed in Ukraine, marking one of the most comprehensive casualty tallies.

item.title: 🚀💰 Elon Musk becomes worlds first trillionaire after SpaceX IPO
item.description: BBC reports Elon Musk became the first trillionaire as SpaceX shares surged in their Nasdaq debut, valuing the company at over $2 trillion and making it the sixth-largest U.S. company.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 US Downs Iranian Drones in Strait of Hormuz as Peace Deal Nears
item.description: The US military shot down multiple Iranian attack drones targeting commercial vessels near the Strait of Hormuz, even as both Washington and Tehran signaled that a comprehensive peace agreement is imminent.

item.title: 🇵🇰🇺🇸🇮🇷 Pakistan Confirms Final Text of US-Iran Peace Deal Reached
item.description: Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif announced that a final, agreed-upon text for a peace deal between the United States and Iran has been reached, with both sides working on implementation steps.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇺🇸 China Arrests US Scholar on Suspicion of Spying
item.description: China confirmed the arrest of Min Zin, a US citizen who leads a think-tank focused on Myanmar, on suspicion of espionage and endangering Chinese national security.

item.title: 🇪🇺🇺🇦 EU Greenlights First Phase of Membership Talks for Ukraine and Moldova
item.description: Ambassadors from all 27 EU countries agreed to advance membership negotiations with Ukraine and Moldova, with the first phase of accession talks set to begin on Monday.

item.title: 🇵🇭 Philippines Death Toll Rises to 61 After Powerful Mindanao Earthquake
item.description: The death toll from the magnitude 7.8 earthquake that struck the southern Philippines has climbed to 61, with disaster agencies continuing rescue and recovery operations across Mindanao.

item.title: 🌡️ Antarctica Records Winter Temperatures 20C Above Normal
item.description: Scientists are observing a "huge anomaly" as Antarctica experiences a prolonged heatwave with temperatures 20 degrees Celsius warmer than normal for winter, raising concerns about accelerating ice loss.

item.title: 🇺🇦💰 Ukraine to request $20 billion from allies to maintain battlefield momentum
item.description: Ukraine is seeking additional military funding from allies to cement its battlefield advantage over Russia, as Kyiv looks to sustain gains in the four-year-old war.

item.title: 🤖🏛️ AI executives from Anthropic, OpenAI, Google to attend G7 summit
item.description: Tech executives will join G7 leaders in France next week to discuss artificial intelligence governance, online safety, and economic challenges.
